questions.question: Who was the first Roman emperor?
questions.explanation: Augustus (born Gaius Octavius) became the first Roman emperor in 27 BCE, ending the Roman Republic and beginning the Roman Empire.

questions.question: Which Roman general crossed the Rubicon river in 49 BCE?
questions.explanation: Julius Caesar crossed the Rubicon with his army in 49 BCE, an act considered treason and triggering the civil war that ended the Roman Republic.

questions.question: What was the main language spoken in ancient Rome?
questions.explanation: Latin was the official language of ancient Rome and the Roman Empire, and it is the ancestor of modern Romance languages.

questions.question: Which structure was used in Rome for gladiatorial combats?
questions.explanation: The Colosseum (or Flavian Amphitheatre) was the largest amphitheatre in the Roman Empire and was used for gladiatorial contests and public spectacles.

questions.question: When did the Western Roman Empire fall?
questions.explanation: The Western Roman Empire is traditionally said to have fallen in 476 CE when the Germanic chieftain Odoacer deposed the last emperor, Romulus Augustulus.

questions.question: Who was the famous orator and philosopher in late Roman Republic?
questions.explanation: Marcus Tullius Cicero was a Roman statesman, orator, lawyer, and philosopher of the late Roman Republic, considered one of Rome's greatest orators.

questions.question: Which emperor legalized Christianity in the Roman Empire?
questions.explanation: Emperor Constantine the Great legalized Christianity in 313 CE through the Edict of Milan, and later played a major role in shaping the religion.

questions.question: What was the Senate in ancient Rome?
options: A governing and advisory political assembly
questions.explanation: The Roman Senate was a political institution that served as an advisory and legislative body during the Republic and Empire.

questions.question: Which Carthaginian general famously crossed the Alps to attack Rome?
questions.explanation: Hannibal Barca led Carthaginian forces, including war elephants, across the Alps in 218 BCE during the Second Punic War.

questions.question: What was the Pax Romana?
options: A period of relative peace and stability in the Roman Empire
questions.explanation: The Pax Romana ('Roman Peace') was a period of relative peace and stability across the Roman Empire lasting approximately 200 years (27 BCE - 180 CE).

questions.question: When did World War 2 begin in Europe?
questions.explanation: World War 2 began in Europe on September 1, 1939, when Nazi Germany invaded Poland.

questions.question: Which event caused the United States to enter World War 2?
questions.explanation: The Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941, prompted the United States to declare war on Japan and enter World War 2.

questions.question: Who was the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom during most of WW2?
questions.explanation: Winston Churchill served as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom from 1940 to 1945, leading the country through most of World War 2.

questions.question: What was the code name for the Allied invasion of Normandy?
questions.explanation: Operation Overlord was the code name for the Battle of Normandy, which began with the D-Day landings on June 6, 1944.

questions.question: When did World War 2 end?
questions.explanation: World War 2 ended in 1945, with V-E Day on May 8, 1945, and V-J Day on September 2, 1945, after Japan's formal surrender.

questions.question: Which two cities were destroyed by atomic bombs in 1945?
questions.explanation: The United States dropped atomic bombs on Hiroshima on August 6, 1945, and Nagasaki on August 9, 1945, leading to Japan's surrender.

questions.question: What was the name of Germany's invasion of the Soviet Union?
questions.explanation: Operation Barbarossa was the code name for the German invasion of the Soviet Union, which began on June 22, 1941.

questions.question: Which leader is associated with the policy of appeasement before WW2?
questions.explanation: Neville Chamberlain, British PM before Churchill, is known for the appeasement policy and the Munich Agreement of 1938.

questions.question: What was the Holocaust?
options: The systematic genocide of six million Jews by Nazi Germany
questions.explanation: The Holocaust was the systematic, state-sponsored persecution and murder of six million Jews and millions of others by Nazi Germany during WW2.

questions.question: Which conference divided post-war Europe into spheres of influence?
questions.explanation: The Yalta Conference of February 1945, attended by Churchill, Roosevelt, and Stalin, shaped post-war Europe and divided it into spheres of influence.
